transcript.text: Hello. This video is what I think about hypocrisy.
Saying something, declaring something, and then not practicing.
There are two, um, or I guess there's one quote having to do with that.
Don't practice what you preach, which people like to say.
And there's another one that also comes to mind, uh, don't bite the hand that feeds you.
I do not like either of those quotes, and I think hypocrisy is good.
Um, some, or hypocrisy is an empty, empty word.
There's nothing wrong with hypocrisy.
Some, um, examples that I can think of right now are, say, uh, uh, Christian, um, preachers.
Who will preach, you know, fidelity and, um, kind of sexual, um, frugality, I guess is the word.
Yet they'll go around and be unfaithful and, uh, rape little kids and stuff.
Um, I don't think the two have anything to do with each other.
I think maybe a lot of people who preach, like a preacher who would preach fidelity, and then not practice that, is probably maybe he's preaching it to try to, uh, help himself not be unfaithful.
Uh, or not be, is that the right word to say?
Yeah.
Um, because, you know, a good first step in trying to heal your sickness would be to vocalize, you know?
Like if somebody's a kleptomaniac, they're, uh, addicted to, uh, stealing all the time, and then they go, uh, stealing's bad, stealing's bad, everybody.
Maybe they're actually saying that to themselves.
And they, but they, but they can't say to themselves because they keep stealing, so they think if they say to everybody else, it'll keep, it'll, it'll help them not steal.
So, if you're telling somebody, calling somebody a hypocrite angrily, um, you're not doing them any, any good.
And you're actually, um, causing harm to people because they're making people afraid of, um, speaking out against, um, their ailments.
So, um, as far as my personal life with this, uh, a couple days ago I uploaded a video talking about how you should sign petitions on the internet about causes you're for,
And, um, put videos of, um, these various crimes against humanity that third world countries do.
And I haven't done any of those things yet.
Yet I preached.
So that makes me a hypocrite.
So what?
Because hypocrites aren't bad.
Because along with being a hypocrite, I'm also a person who preaches what I believe.
And it's more important to preach what you believe than worry about being a hypocrite.
So, hypocrisy, nothing wrong with it.
Hypocrisy only kind of, I think hypocrisy has a bad name because people confuse it with lying.
Um, but people aren't, it's not lying when you say you shouldn't, uh, people shouldn't, um, be unfaithful.
What's, what's lying is if you say I am unfaithful and you should be like me.
But, uh, that's totally different.
So, and as far as bite the, uh, the, uh, hand that feeds you, um, it's better to just, um, use, uh, specific examples.
That's just very vague.
Uh, and it's okay to speak out, you know, sometimes, like your parents.
Kids bite the hand that feed them all the time.
And, uh, so everybody does that.
So there's nothing wrong with it.
So, hypocrisy's good.
